solution: 
class Solution:
    def memLeak(self, memory1: int, memory2: int) -> List[int]:
        time = 1
        memory1_alloc = 0
        memory2_alloc = 0
        while memory1 > 0 and memory2 > 0:
            if memory1 >= memory2:
                memory1_alloc += time
                memory1 -= time
            else:
                memory2_alloc += time
                memory2 -= time
            time += 1
        return [time, memory1, memory2]

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a simple while loop to simulate the memory allocation process. It keeps track of the available memory on both sticks and the time at which the program crashes. The time complexity is O(1) because the loop runs a constant number of times, and the space complexity is O(1) because only a constant amount of space is used.
